User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the FitCoin platform, primarily identified by their wallet address and storing their personal fitness-related data and progress.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, which is the user's Ethereum wallet address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user first interacted with the FitCoin platform.

lastActivityAt.description: Timestamp of the user's most recent activity (e.g., logged workout, purchase, dashboard visit).

totalWorkoutsCompleted.description: The total number of workout sessions completed by the user.

totalFitEarned.description: The cumulative total of FIT tokens earned by the user across all workouts and bonuses, for local display and leaderboard calculations. This may differ from the actual on-chain balance due to spending.

currentStreakDays.description: The number of consecutive days the user has completed at least one workout.

lastWorkoutDate.description: The date (without time component) of the user's most recent workout, used for calculating workout streaks.

hasClaimedFirstWorkoutBonus.description: Flag indicating whether the user has received the one-time welcome bonus for their very first workout.

unlockedAchievementIds.description: An array of unique identifiers for Achievement entities that this user has unlocked. (Relationship: User N:N Achievement)

WorkoutSession.title: WorkoutSession
WorkoutSession.description: Records the details of a single workout session completed by a user, including type, duration, and tokens earned.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkoutSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed this workout session. (Relationship: User 1:N WorkoutSession)

workoutType.description: The category or type of workout performed (e.g., 'Cardio', 'Strength', 'Yoga').

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the workout session in minutes.

startTime.description: The timestamp indicating when the workout session began.

endTime.description: The timestamp indicating when the workout session ended.

baseTokensEarned.description: The base amount of FIT tokens earned from this workout session, calculated solely based on duration.

bonusTokensEarned.description: The total additional FIT tokens earned from applicable bonuses (e.g., early bird, weekend, streak) for this session.

totalTokensEarned.description: The sum of base and bonus tokens earned for this workout session, representing the total expected reward before on-chain transfer.

appliedBonuses.description: A list of identifiers for the specific bonus types that were applied to this workout session (e.g., 'EarlyBird', 'Weekend', 'StreakBonus').

transactionHash.description: Optional: The blockchain transaction hash if the token reward for this workout was successfully processed on-chain.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Defines an item available for purchase in the FitCoin rewards shop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The display name of the reward product.

description.description: A brief description or detail about the product.

emoji.description: An emoji associated with the product for visual representation in the UI.

category.description: The category to which the product belongs (e.g., 'Nutrition', 'Equipment', 'Apparel', 'Training').

priceInFit.description: The cost of the product, denominated in FIT tokens.

imageUrl.description: Optional: URL to an image asset for the product.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Records a specific instance of a user purchasing a product from the rewards shop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Purchase entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this purchase. (Relationship: User 1:N Purchase)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was purchased. (Relationship: Product 1:N Purchase)

purchaseDate.description: The timestamp when the product purchase occurred.

fitTokensSpent.description: The amount of FIT tokens spent on this specific purchase.

transactionHash.description: Optional: The blockchain transaction hash if the token expenditure for this purchase was recorded on-chain.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Defines the criteria and details for an achievement that users can unlock within the FitCoin app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

name.description: The display name of the achievement (e.g., '7-Day Streak Master').

description.description: A detailed explanation of what the achievement entails.

iconUrl.description: URL to an image or icon representing the achievement.

criteriaDescription.description: A human-readable description of the conditions required to unlock this achievement (e.g., 'Complete 7 consecutive daily workouts').

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the user's authenticated wallet address (`request.auth.uid`). This provides private, path-based ownership for the user's core data.

Records individual workout sessions for a specific user. Nested under the user's document for direct ownership and simplified security rules. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this workout session, corresponding to their authenticated wallet address.

params.name: workoutSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific workout session.

definition.description: Contains definitions of products available in the rewards shop. This is a global, publicly readable collection, ensuring easy listing for all users in the shop UI.
Records purchase transactions made by a user. Nested under the user's document for private, path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made this purchase, corresponding to their authenticated wallet address.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific purchase transaction.

definition.description: Contains definitions of all achievements available in the FitCoin app. This is a global, publicly readable collection for all users to view available and unlocked achievements.

Admin roles are managed here. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the `adminUid` (which matches `request.auth.uid`) has administrative privileges, enabling DBAC without custom claims or `get()` operations. The schema refers to 'User' as it represents an administrative user.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who has been granted administrative access.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure for FitCoin prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules. All user-specific and sensitive data (User profiles, Workout Sessions, Purchases) are nested under `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership is crucial for Authorization Independence. For example, a user's workout sessions are stored at `/users/{userId}/workoutSessions/{workoutSessionId}`. In this structure, the `userId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, eliminating the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check ownership. This makes rule writing simple and explicit (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`). This design also inherently supports atomic operations as related user data can be created, updated, or deleted without cross-document `get()` dependencies for authorization.

Publicly accessible data, such as `Product` listings and `Achievement` definitions, are stored in top-level collections (`/products` and `/achievements`). These collections have a homogeneous security posture (globally readable), which simplifies security rules significantly. This segregation ensures that `list` operations (QAPs) on these collections are secure and efficient, as they do not require complex filtering logic in rules; instead, all documents are intended to be publicly viewable.

For administrative access, a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` is introduced. Authorization for admin actions will rely on the existence of a document in this collection matching `request.auth.uid`, following the 'Existence over Content' principle for DBAC and further enhancing Authorization Independence. This structure completely avoids custom claims and `get()` calls for role checks.

All wildcards (`{userId}`, `{workoutSessionId}`, etc.) are descriptive, improving clarity and maintainability. The design ensures that each collection maintains a consistent security posture, moving complexity from rule logic to the data structure itself, which is a core principle for debuggability and scalability.
